channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Evidence Grows That One of the Largest Known Stars Is Poised to Explode in a Spectacular Blast - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Evidence Grows That One of the Largest Known Stars Is Poised to Explode in a Spectacular Blast  Yahoo
  2. The dramatic transition of the extreme red supergiant WOH G64 to a yellow hypergiant  Nature
  3. Astronomers just watched a star 1,540 times the size of our sun transform into a hypergiant. Will it go supernova?  Space
  4. A well-known and widely studied star changed from red to yellow in a timeframe that is "too fast" for current models, and astronomers admit they still can't fully explain what happened.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. Changes to One of The Largest Known Stars May Signal Destruction  ScienceAlert

item.title: MeerKAT discovers record-breaking cosmic laser halfway across the universe - Phys.org
item.description:

  3. MeerKAT uncovers record-setting cosmic laser in a galaxy 8 billion light-years away  starlust.org
  4. Meet the Gigamaser—the Brightest Microwave Laser Ever Spotted in Deep Space  Gizmodo
  5. A microwave laser has been discovered nearly 8 billion light-years away and breaks a historic record by emitting a signal of 1667 megahertz during the collision of two galaxies.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
